items.front: Five operative words and their meanings in this order.
items.back: Must = mandatory; Must not = prohibited; Should = recommended; May/need not = optional; Will = futurity (not a requirement).

items.front: What does 'aircraft' mean in this order?
items.back: The airframe, crew members, or both. It does not exclusively mean just the physical airframe.
Separation in accordance with the applicable minima in this order.

items.front: What does 'miles' mean in this order?
items.back: Nautical miles unless otherwise specified; statute miles in conjunction with visibility.

items.front: Are course, bearing, heading, and wind direction magnetic or true by default?
items.back: Always magnetic unless specifically stated otherwise.

items.front: ATC time convention vs. time check convention under 1-2-1m.
items.back: Standard: change to next minute at minute + 30 seconds. Time checks: given to the nearest quarter minute. Both in UTC.
Not unless otherwise specified. Runway means the runway used by aircraft and does not include helipads or their takeoff/landing courses.
No. The note in 1-2-1 explicitly states a pilot's use of 'Going Tactical' does not indicate Due Regard.

items.front: Two obligations of a state aircraft commander under due regard/operational options.
items.back: 1) Separate from all other air traffic. 2) Assure an appropriate monitoring agency assumes SAR responsibility. Plus one of four conditions (VMC, surveillance+comms, airborne radar, or Class G).

items.front: Three course classifications and their angular ranges.
items.back: Same: < 45°. Crossing: 45°–135° inclusive. Opposite/reciprocal: > 135°–180° inclusive. Same and opposite also require protected airspaces to be coincident, overlap, or intersect.
Airspace equal to one-half the required applicable lateral separation on either side of an aircraft along its projected flight path. Non-overlapping protected airspace = lateral separation ensured.

items.front: What do bold vertical lines in the margin mean?
items.back: The location of substantive revisions. Adjacent to a title = extensive changes to that chapter, section, or paragraph.

items.front: What do EN ROUTE, OCEANIC, or TERMINAL annotations mean?
items.back: The paragraph applies only to that type of facility. Without these annotations, paragraphs apply to all facility types.

items.front: What does USAF, USN, or USA annotation mean?
items.back: The procedure immediately following applies only to that designated military service. Each annotation is service-exclusive.

items.front: What does WAKE TURBULENCE APPLICATION mean?
items.back: The remaining information in the paragraph requires the application of wake turbulence procedures — not just the next sentence.

items.front: Can a controller rephrase after using prescribed phraseology?
items.back: Yes. After first using the prescribed phraseology, controllers may rephrase to ensure understanding, using good judgment with nonstandard wording.

items.front: Is an EXAMPLE without preceding prescribed phraseology mandatory verbatim?
items.back: No. The exact text is not mandatory, but wording should approximate the example to the extent practical.
